title: Layers and Secrets
description:
## A Deleted File That Isn't Gone

A Dockerfile does this:

```dockerfile
COPY secret.key /secret.key
RUN ./use-key.sh
RUN rm /secret.key
```

Why is shipping this image still a security problem, even though the file is removed?

options.text: It isn't — the final `RUN rm` deletes the file, so the image is clean
Each instruction creates a layer; the earlier layer that added `secret.key` still contains it and can be extracted from the image history
The problem is only that the build is slow
`rm` inside a container never actually deletes files

explanation: Image layers are stacked and immutable. The `COPY secret.key` layer permanently records the file; a later `RUN rm` only adds a *new* layer that hides it in the final filesystem view. Anyone can `docker save`/`docker history` the image and recover the secret from the earlier layer. Fixes: never copy secrets into images, use multi-stage builds so secrets stay in a discarded stage, or use BuildKit `--secret` mounts which never persist to a layer.

hints.title: Inspect the layers
hints.body: docker history shows the instruction and size of every layer that makes up an image.
